🎯 Management Attention

generated 2026-07-17 10:39 UTC · guidelines: docs/incident-handling-guidelines.md
Under control?No — 2x 🚨 (INC-8064 H2Bet duplicate bonuses, unchanged 50+ days, REW-176 parked until mid-Aug; INC-12246 Lottomart security incident, newly escalated — P1 silent 9 days with full-brand rollout never confirmed) and 8x 🔴 need attention; 1 resolved clean (Betpipo), 1 new (fresh RDS CPU alert on royal-reels, too early to assess)
Urgent vs smaller2 urgent (🚨), 8 need a nudge (🔴), 18 watch-level (🟡), 5 well-handled (🟢)
Total open (active, excl. triage)33 +0 vs last digest (33 vs 33) -- 1 new, 1 resolved
2
🚨 Escalate now
8
🔴 Needs attention
18
🟡 Watch
5
🟢 Under control

🆕 New since last digest

  • INC-14486 — P2 AWS RDS CPU royal-reels; instant ack+lead, handler flags it as a daily recurrence (A4) to watch

✅ Resolved since last digest

  • INC-11707 — Betpipo repeated-logout — closed clean; root cause (UserTokens collision) fixed 15 Jul, ~24h quiet observation window passed with no recurrence
Guideline codes legend (docs/incident-handling-guidelines.md)
  • A1 — Every incident has an owner and Head-of-SRE-level visibility
  • A2 — No incident is “just closed” — every problem gets an action
  • A3 — “It's just 10/30 minutes” is never an acceptable rationale
  • A4 — Recurring problems are escalated to the owning team for a long-term fix
  • B5 — Escalate early — not after hours without recovery
  • B6 — Security incidents are P1 until de-escalated
  • B7 — Critical-path failures are top priority
  • B8 — Night and handover incidents must not be lost — assign, don't just mention
  • C9 — Inform partners fast, even before root cause is known
  • C10 — Close the loop — recovery comms sent, partner questions answered
  • C11 — Comms accurate and crystal clear on impact
  • D12 — Informed decisions over passive waiting
  • D13 — Runbooks followed, basic checks done
  • D14 — Don't close while partner-side work remains open
  • D15 — The incident record is self-contained and phone-readable
  • E16 — Digest answers: under control? urgent vs smaller? new bugs? how many open?

🚨 Escalate now 2

INC-8064 slack intercomP1open 51d· evidence to 09 Jul 09:04 UTC
[P1] Intercom Incident : H2Bet Production
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: correction — H2Bet ACCEPTED the interim workaround (discussed in the meeting with them and our Partner Manager, early Jul); that acceptance is why REW-176 was opened on 9 Jul. The 28 May rejection was the earlier 503-as-success proposal only. No sign-off is pending from H2Bet.

~300 duplicate bonus grants/week continuing after 50+ days; the accepted interim workaround (REW-176) is parked until mid-Aug by Rewards capacity, and the BetConstruct root-cause path has zero traction.

H2Bet duplicate bonus grants confirmed root cause quickly, but 50+ days later still ~300 duplicate grants/week with no fix deployed. Per management note, H2Bet accepted the interim workaround (REW-176), opened 9 Jul, but it remains blocked by Rewards capacity until mid-Aug. No change this cycle — 0 new Slack messages, 0 new Intercom parts, situation fully static, financial leakage continuing unabated.

Open breaches
  • A2/A4: no decisive fix after 50+ days, Urgent ticket parked a month+
  • B5: BetConstruct escalation zero traction
  • C10/D15: Intercom closed (since 06 Jul) implying resolution while incident.io still open P1
Proposed / pending
  • REW-176 interim workaround - ACCEPTED by H2Bet (per management note), opened Urgent 9 Jul, blocked by Rewards capacity until mid-Aug
Timeline (2)
  • No new Slack or Intercom activity this cycle
  • No new activity this cycle -- ~300 duplicate grants/week continuing, REW-176 still parked until mid-Aug

→ Decide this week: pull REW-176 forward in Rewards' queue, or formally document risk-acceptance of the leakage until mid-Aug; separately escalate the BetConstruct account-manager gap to leadership.

INC-12246 slackP1open 13d· evidence to 17 Jul 07:22 UTC
Exploitable Image found in Lottomart Privileged Kubernetes Container

A genuine (not false-positive) P1 security exposure has gone silent for 9 days with no confirmation the fix was rolled out beyond Lottomart, despite Patrik explicitly asking for full-brand closure — this is exactly the B6/A2 pattern management wants surfaced, not left to quietly age.

Wiz found a privileged, internet-facing Kubernetes container (ambassador) in Lottomart production with exploitable CVEs (vulnerable curl dependency). Root cause identified fast (04-06 Jul) and a fix (PR #20, replace curl with Python) was tested and rolled out to Lottomart by 06 Jul. Patrik explicitly asked that the fix be rolled out to all other brands too ('close those also if there are new ones and we release and update to all'). The last substantive update was 08 Jul ('rolling out the fixed image on this PV') — since then, 9 days of complete silence in the channel, still shown as P1/Investigating, with no confirmation the rollout ever reached brands beyond Lottomart.

Open breaches
  • B6/A2: P1 security incident silent for 9 days with the incident.io record still 'Investigating' — no evidence the Lottomart-only fix was ever extended to other brands as Patrik asked
  • D15: the incident record itself only has 2 status updates (03 Jul, 06 Jul) and doesn't reflect the 08 Jul rollout message or current state at all — not understandable from the record alone
Proposed / pending
  • Replace curl with Python in Ambassador image (PR #20) — tested and deployed to Lottomart 08 Jul; rollout to remaining brands never confirmed
Timeline (6)
  • 03 Jul 21:29 UTC incident opened from a Wiz finding, escalated same night, Joachim → Agustín as lead
  • 06 Jul 06:25 UTC formal update: root cause (curl CVEs) confirmed, PR #20 opened, plan to test then roll out Lottomart-first then other brands
  • 04 Jul: Patrik explicitly asks that once released, similar CVEs on other brands also get closed out as part of the same rollout
  • 08 Jul 07:17-07:18 CEST: Agustín says the fixed image is being rolled out on 'this PV' (a stable-platform-version release train) — last substantive message
  • 08 Jul 07:48/09:18 CEST: two automated check-ins asking if it can be marked resolved — no human reply
  • 17 Jul 09:22 CEST: Andrea Azzopardi joins the channel (passive join only, no content) — first activity in 9 days

→ Get Agustín (or current owner — Andrea just joined the channel today) to confirm rollout status across all brands and post a closing update, or formally re-baseline the incident with current state.

🔴 Needs attention 8

INC-12617 slackP2open 9d· evidence to 16 Jul 02:02 UTC
[FIRING:3] Fusion API errors Alerting (true)

Andrea's intervention today landed on the recurrence (INC-13895) rather than this original incident — worth making sure the pattern-level escalation Andrea asked for covers both.

Fusion API 401 errors; root cause ruled out as FT-side, partner informed via Teams DM 07 Jul and never re-chased since. No new Slack activity and no incident.io change at all this cycle — now roughly 9.5 days of total silence. The exact 401 pattern has recurred as INC-13895, which today got direct management attention from Andrea (see that incident) — this original occurrence has not.

Open breaches
  • C9: no partner re-chase in over 9 days
  • C10: alert cleared nearly two weeks ago, never formally closed
  • A4: recurring Fusion API 401 pattern (2nd occurrence INC-13895, now getting attention) still not escalated as a pattern from this side
Proposed / pending
  • log investigation, rule out FT-side config - done
  • inform partner - done once, never re-chased
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-07T14:19
  • last partner message unanswered
Timeline (1)
  • No new Slack messages this cycle

→ When escalating the Fusion API 401 pattern (per Andrea's direction on INC-13895), close this incident out explicitly as part of the same pattern rather than leaving it separately unresolved.

INC-13738 slackP2open 3d· evidence to 16 Jul 04:45 UTC
Betiro - No unsubs since April 2026

Unchanged since last check — the technical fix and monitor are in, but the compliance-exposure items (legal escalation, retroactive remediation, 3 new brands) still have no owner or movement.

IP-whitelisting change silently blocked unsubscribe requests for Betiro, Rivalo, Leonbets since 7 Apr - undetected ~3 months. Fix and the specifically-requested unsub-events monitor (PR #196) both landed 13-15 Jul. No new activity this cycle. Still open: no legal/compliance escalation for the ~3-month opt-out failure, no confirmed retroactive remediation, and 3 newly-flagged brands (Rocketship, Fairspin, Frank Casino) remain unaddressed.

Open breaches
  • D12: still no evidence of legal/compliance escalation for a multi-month opt-out failure across 3 gambling brands
  • C10: no confirmed retroactive remediation of missed unsubscribes
  • 3 newly-flagged brands remain unaddressed
Proposed / pending
  • Monitor landed (PR #196) - addresses detection gap
  • Open: retroactive remediation plan
  • Open: legal/compliance escalation
  • Open: extend investigation to Rocketship, Fairspin, Frank Casino
Timeline (1)
  • No new Slack messages this cycle

→ Escalate the opt-out failure to legal/compliance given regulatory exposure; confirm/execute retroactive remediation; open tracking for the 3 newly-flagged brands before treating this as closed.

INC-13917 slack intercomP2open 2d· evidence to 17 Jul 01:21 UTC
Campeon: Slow processing on credit service (from lifecycles)

The partner side is now fully resolved and closed, which makes the still-blank incident.io record more conspicuous, not less — there's a real risk this gets forgotten precisely because the visible (Intercom) side already looks done.

Campeon lifecycle coin-crediting delayed up to 2h on 11 Jul, root cause auto-claim + serialized credit path. An 8xlarge instance upgrade completed as short-term mitigation (~13:35 CEST 15 Jul); partner was told and confirmed via Intercom, ticket formally closed 16 Jul (rated by partner). No new Slack activity this cycle. incident.io itself has never been updated at all — still shows 'Investigating' with zero status updates despite the mitigation, partner comms, and even the Intercom-side closure having all already happened.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io still shows zero status updates and 'Investigating' even though the partner-facing Intercom ticket is already closed and CSAT-rated — the internal record is now behind the partner-facing one
  • C11: partner told a generic 'performance bottleneck'; internally-confirmed root cause still not disclosed
  • D14: permanent fix (credit VC directly vs auto-claim) still undecided while the record trends toward looking closed
Proposed / pending
  • Temporary: scale credit-service compute to 8xlarge - completed
  • Permanent: credit VC directly vs auto-claim bonus - undecided
  • Add audit trail for Rewards bonus-config edits - raised, not tracked
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-14T11:18
  • Rewards team confirmed a performance-bottleneck backlog - partial/generic explanation
  • Infrastructure upgrade confirmed complete to partner - delivered, ticket closed
Timeline (4)
  • ~13:35 CEST 15 Jul 8xlarge instance upgrade completed
  • Intercom ticket closed and CSAT-rated 16 Jul (~09:16 UTC) — partner side is fully wrapped up
  • incident.io still shows zero status updates and status 'Investigating'
  • No new activity this cycle -- incident.io still shows zero status updates and 'Investigating' despite the partner side being fully closed and CSAT-rated since 16 Jul

→ Post the overdue incident.io status update now that the partner ticket has closed, and make an explicit decision on the permanent fix (direct VC credit vs auto-claim) rather than leaving it open indefinitely.

INC-14151 slack intercomP2open 45h· evidence to 17 Jul 00:03 UTC
[P2] Intercom Incident : MeridianBet Production

The verification request has now gone unanswered for nearly a full day and the previously-suggested nudge never went out -- escalated from 🟡 to 🔴.

MeridianBet couldn't add sports bonuses; Hikmet found the real root cause (hidden required AuthType field) and fixed it directly. Joachim asked the partner via Intercom at 08:39 UTC to re-check if it's resolved. No reply from the partner and no new Slack/Intercom activity since — the re-check request has now been outstanding for ~6 hours.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io's logged update still describes the superseded first FE fix, not the real root cause
  • C10: partner re-check request now ~19.5 hours unanswered, and the suggested follow-up nudge from last digest was not sent
Proposed / pending
  • Actual fix (Hikmet): remove 'required' flag from AuthType field - applied, unverified with partner
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-15T11:58
  • last partner message unanswered
  • Partner re-check requested 08:39 UTC 16 Jul - no reply after ~6 hours
Timeline (4)
  • 15 Jul 19:34 UTC Hikmet finds and fixes the real root cause (AuthType field required+hidden, no default)
  • 16 Jul 08:36-08:39 CEST Joachim confirms no deployment needed, asks partner (Jamie) via Intercom to re-check
  • No reply or further activity since (checked again 16:39 CEST — ~8h outstanding)
  • As of this check (02:09 UTC 17 Jul) the partner re-check request (asked 08:39 CEST 16 Jul) remains unanswered ~19.5 hours on -- no follow-up nudge was sent despite last digest's suggested action, and incident.io still describes the superseded first fix

→ Send the partner follow-up nudge now (overdue since yesterday); update incident.io with the real root cause (AuthType field) regardless of partner reply timing.

INC-14177 slackP2open 41h· evidence to 16 Jul 19:26 UTC
campeon: no consumers on queue campeon.actions.callbackconsumer (messages: 1, in: 0/s)

Multiple guideline gaps now compounding: the partner push Joachim promised ~18h ago is still unconfirmed in-channel (C9), the original off-platform partner comms were never captured (D15), and the same queue has now recurred at least twice more through the day with no root-cause fix in sight (A4) -- escalated from 🟡 to 🔴.

A single stuck message on the campeon callback-consumer queue triggered a P2 alert; James acknowledged instantly but the incident sat in Triage ~1h43m before moving to Investigating, then jumped straight to Waiting on Partner via a colleague's off-platform Teams relay. Lead reassigned twice overnight, now Joachim, who said this morning (08:24 CEST) he'd 'send a push this morning' to the partner — no new channel activity since, so as of this check (14:39 CEST) that push has not visibly happened.

Open breaches
  • D15: record not self-contained - partner comms happened off-platform via a colleague with no content/timestamp captured
  • B5: original ~1h43m quiet gap between alert and investigation start
  • C9: the morning partner push Joachim committed to at 08:24 CEST has not appeared in-channel roughly 6 hours later
Proposed / pending
  • Escalate to partner via Teams (delegated to Gian) - done, but undocumented in-channel
Partner view
  • last partner message unanswered
  • Partner message relayed via Teams by Gian on James's behalf - content/timestamp not captured in the incident channel
  • Joachim: partner push planned this morning (16 Jul) - still not visible in-channel as of 14:39 CEST
Timeline (9)
  • 15 Jul 16:44 UTC alert fires (1 message stuck), James acknowledges within seconds
  • 15 Jul 18:27 UTC (~1h43m later) incident accepted, Triage -> Investigating
  • 15 Jul 18:28 UTC moved to Waiting on Partner; partner message relayed via Teams by a colleague on James's behalf
  • 16 Jul 05:54-08:10 CEST Incident Lead reassigned James -> Nazareno -> Joachim Farrugia
  • 16 Jul 08:24 CEST Joachim: 'Waiting on them' (partner); 'Will send a push this morning' — no further channel activity since
  • 16 Jul 14:57 CEST James Hodgson asks to be added to the channel; 15:00 CEST bot check-in nudge — still no sign the promised partner push happened
  • 16 Jul 19:37 CEST another related incident (INC-14377) auto-merged in, 6 more alerts attached
  • 16 Jul 21:26 CEST yet another related incident (INC-14405) auto-merged in -- same queue stalling repeatedly through the day (A4 recurrence signal)
  • No human message addressing the still-unconfirmed morning partner push (promised 08:24 CEST 16 Jul, ~18h ago) or capturing the off-platform Teams comms

→ Joachim/James to confirm in-channel whether the promised partner push actually went out, and to look at why this queue keeps stalling (2 more merges today) rather than treating each recurrence as a one-off.

Unchanged — the textbook long-runner-with-no-action-plan case: a confirmed root cause and cheap fix list sitting untouched on a recurring failure class.

INC-14301 slackP3open 26h· evidence to 17 Jul 06:33 UTC

Escalating further: not just a missing closing update anymore -- the assigned lead has left the channel entirely with the incident still open and undocumented, ~26.5h after the backlog cleared.

Star Casino NL bonus-distributor queue backlog (25,904 messages). Giancarlo accepted and sent partner comms within ~20 minutes, noting the partner's own website was down. Both attached alerts have since resolved (queue drained) as of 11:49 CEST, but incident.io status has not moved off 'Investigating' since 08:29:46 CEST — over 4 hours with no closing update despite the underlying issue clearing.

Open breaches
  • C10/D15: both underlying alerts resolved 11:49 CEST 16 Jul but incident.io still shows 'Investigating' ~26.5 hours later; the assigned lead (Giancarlo) has now left the channel without posting a closing update

  • 16 Jul 08:09 UTC alert fires (25,904-message backlog)
  • 08:29 CEST Giancarlo accepts, notes partner's website is down, sends comms
  • 09:24 CEST first attached alert resolves; 11:49 CEST second attached alert resolves — queue fully drained
  • No status update since 08:33:57 CEST despite both alerts clearing
  • As of this check (02:09 UTC 17 Jul) still 'Investigating' with zero closing update ~18h after both alerts cleared (11:49 CEST 16 Jul) -- only a bot nudge (19:30 CEST) since, unanswered
  • 17 Jul 08:33 CEST Giancarlo Laferla leaves the incident channel -- no closing update posted; incident.io still shows 'Investigating', now ~26.5h since both alerts cleared (11:49 CEST 16 Jul)

→ Reassign the incident or have Giancarlo return to post the closing update now: confirm the backlog cleared, what partner comms said, and whether the partner's own outage (root cause) is confirmed resolved.

🟡 Watch 18

INC-12022 slack intercomP1open 14d· evidence to 14 Jul 12:42 UTC
Shortlinks Redirecting to "Forbidden" page
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: Solved as an incident. Only pending item is with Estelarbet, who need to identify which players were impacted — this is being checked and shared from our side; we need their input on which players complained so we can cross-check. Confirmed via the partner Intercom ticket.

Per management note: solved as an incident, only the Estelarbet cross-check was pending -- Giancarlo has now started that cross-check today using the complainant ID the partner supplied (traces to a test account).

Cloudflare-origin security check blocked legitimate shortlink/unsubscribe traffic on 2 Jul; mitigated within ~2h, root-cause fix merged same evening. Partner (Estelarbet/PLIN) has been waiting on a promised RCA report through several broken promises; Giancarlo re-chased the partner again today (13:32 CEST) for the complainant details needed to close the loop.

Open breaches
  • C10: impact cross-check + final report pending — awaiting partner's complainant list (requested 15 Jul)
  • D15: incident.io status timestamps misrepresent when fix landed
  • A4: same CF/unsub-blocking pattern recurring across 3 incidents (also see INC-13738)
Proposed / pending

  • file-serving revert - merged 02 Jul
  • deliver full partner report - still pending
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-02T13:29
  • RCA/report promised repeatedly (13, 14, 15 Jul), still not delivered
Timeline (3)
  • 16 Jul 13:32 UTC Giancarlo chases the partner again via Intercom: still pending feedback on the reporting user/email needed to investigate the complainant list
  • 16 Jul 20:51 CEST partner (Gloria) replies with the complainant user ID (366366) -- turns out to be a test account running tests, not a real complainant
  • 17 Jul 08:07 UTC Giancarlo replies to Gloria's complainant ID via Intercom: 'I will have a look now' -- cross-check actively started

→ Giancarlo to complete the cross-check (likely no real impact, test account) and send the final report to Estelarbet to close the loop.

INC-9820 slackP2open 31d· evidence to 14 Jul 16:01 UTC
RDS OOM all brands - gradually increasing overtime

Steady, no regressions; only the resilience-hardening follow-up still lacks an owner.

Fleet-wide RDS Performance Schema memory leak, fixed and rolled out fleet-wide by 9 Jul. Follow-ups mostly closed out; only the activity-manager-go resilience follow-up remains outstanding and unassigned. No new activity this cycle.

Open breaches
  • A4: one follow-up (activity-manager-go resilience) remains outstanding and unassigned
Proposed / pending
  • Completed: cap performance_schema memory (PR #416)
  • In progress: RDS/Aurora memory & swap alarm thresholds (assigned)
  • Outstanding, unassigned: bounded retry/backoff for activity-manager-go on transient MySQL OOM errors
Timeline (2)
  • No new Slack activity this cycle
  • No new activity this cycle -- activity-manager-go resilience follow-up still unassigned

→ Assign an owner to the activity-manager-go resilience follow-up before this incident is closed out.

INC-10045 slack intercomP2open 28d· evidence to 17 Jul 09:55 UTC
[P2] Intercom Incident : Power Play Production

Continued thorough, collaborative technical investigation (Matteo/Stephen) into event-ordering reliability — no breach, still resolving a genuine data-modeling question before deciding on a fix.

GiG (Power Play's upstream KYC) event-ordering bug leaves stale block tags. Andrea sent Matteo's drafted handover to Integration/PM (Robert Friberg, Lis, Gui Furst) this morning, framing it as needing a permanent dev fix. Robert pushed back that GiG should fix their own event ordering rather than FT building custom logic, but asked SRE/Integration to scope how big a change the timestamp-based (update_epoch) fix would be as a fallback.

Proposed / pending
  • historical re-sync - done multiple rounds
  • update_epoch as ordering key - drafted handover identifies this as a candidate fix for Integration/dev to assess, with an honest feasibility writeup
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-16T12:16
  • notify once re-syncs complete - kept
  • structural handover to Integration/PM drafted 16 Jul - awaiting Andrea's go-ahead to send
Timeline (5)
  • 16 Jul 10:03 CEST Matteo posts a fully-drafted partner message and an internal SRE→Integration/PM handover memo (root cause, options, open decisions), asks Andrea for sign-off
  • 16 Jul 14:58-15:13 CEST Matteo tells partner (Anna) a correction pass for accounts affected since 30 Jun is coming, and that he'll regroup internally with Lis/Integration on the long-term fix -- partner satisfied for now ('sounds great, thanks')
  • 17 Jul 07:58 CEST Andrea sends the full SRE->Integration/PM handover to Robert Friberg, Lis, and Gui Furst -- root cause, options, and the ask, as drafted by Matteo yesterday
  • 17 Jul 10:22 CEST Robert Friberg: prefers pushing GiG to fix event ordering on their end rather than FT building custom logic, but asks Stephen/Curt to scope the size of the update_epoch-based fallback fix and whether FT has done this before elsewhere
  • 17 Jul 10:57-11:55 CEST: Stephen and Matteo continue rigorous joint analysis of GiG update_epoch reliability for reordering assign/unassign events; conclusion reached that update_epoch (not entry_timestamp) is reliable for ordering since opposite-polarity events for the same tag are never within the same second, despite ~30% of events losing sub-second precision

→ Await Stephen/Curt's scoping answer to Robert, then get a final decision on GiG escalation vs. FT-side timestamp fix.

INC-11079 slackP2open 20d· evidence to 15 Jul 10:46 UTC
segurobet: no consumers on queue segurobet.consentsmanager (messages: 1, in: 0/s)
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: this is in reality a BetConstruct platform issue (their Operator API returns 500 on consent updates). The partner is aware and has raised it with BetConstruct themselves; active partner correspondence (examples provided, regular chasing incl. by Andrea) lives in the partner channel thread: https://fasttrack-solutions.slack.com/archives/C08VB1RBJLF/p1782549375502499 — future runs should treat that thread as the live track, not the quiet incident channel.

Per management note: BetConstruct-side root cause, active partner correspondence, blocked on the vendor not FT inaction. Andrea's fresh re-chase today confirms the cadence is being kept up as intended.

Segurobet Consent Manager queue stalled since 27 Jun due to partner's (BetConstruct/Betco) Operator API 500s. Per Andrea's management note, the live track is the partner channel thread (#ftcrm-segurobet), not the quiet incident channel. Andrea personally re-chased BetConstruct again today (12:54 CEST) in that thread, continuing the active chase cadence — no reply yet.

Open breaches
  • External blocker: BetConstruct 'at a loss' — no ETA; Andrea is actively maintaining the chase cadence
Proposed / pending
  • notify partner + supply failing examples - done
  • no FT-side workaround proposed
  • zero incident.io follow-ups exist
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-27T08:36
  • 'we'll check' (01 Jul) - no ETA ever given; Andrea re-chased again 16 Jul
Timeline (3)
  • 16 Jul 12:54 CEST Andrea Azzopardi re-chases BetConstruct (Eduardo Nunes) directly in the partner thread: 'Are there any updates from Betconstruct about this issue please?' — no reply yet
  • No new activity in the main incident Slack channel this cycle
  • Checked the live partner thread (#ftcrm-segurobet) directly: no reply since Andrea's 12:54 CEST re-chase of BetConstruct

→ Consider an FT-side mitigation while BetConstruct investigates: locally suppress Email/SMS sends for players whose opt-out failed to sync (D12) — removes the compliance exposure without waiting on the vendor.

INC-12998 slackP2open 7d· evidence to 16 Jul 10:47 UTC
frank-casino: deployment ping-pong is stuck on CrashLoopBackOff
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: the deployment ping-pong service is not critical — a crash-loop here is not a critical-path failure (B7 does not apply). Watch-level, not escalate.

Watch-level per management note (ping-pong is non-critical, B7 doesn't apply) — and now has real forward motion: Andrea's own diagnostic work found a concrete error signature (403 on ping) after ~6 days of no diagnostic at all.

frank-casino ping-pong deployment stuck in CrashLoopBackOff. Andrea Azzopardi personally joined the channel today (12:42 CEST) and posted diagnostic logs showing a concrete new lead: the pod is fataling on 'code [403] received from integration API on ping request'. Andrea asked Giancarlo for a summary when he has time, explicitly noting he's aware this isn't a critical service.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io summary field still blank; the 403 finding is in Slack only, not yet in incident.io
Timeline (1)
  • 16 Jul 12:42-12:47 CEST Andrea joins the channel, posts pod logs showing a 403 from the integration API on the ping request, asks Giancarlo for a summary 'when you have time'

→ Giancarlo to follow up on Andrea's 403-integration-API finding and post a summary to incident.io.

INC-13895 slackP2open 3d· evidence to 17 Jul 08:32 UTC
[FIRING:1] Fusion API errors Alerting (spinbet-terragon: 401 @ /external/pusher/:brand true)

Andrea's direction from yesterday (investigate further, correct partner comms) has now been fully actioned: root cause identified precisely (brand slug mismatch) and accurate corrected comms sent to the partner this morning -- downgraded 🔴→🟡. Only the cross-incident pattern escalation (A4, also affecting INC-12617) remains open.

Fusion API 401 errors blocking on-site notifications/Rich Inbox for Spinbet Terragon -- 2nd occurrence of this alert family in a week (see INC-12617). Following Andrea's direction yesterday, Matteo fully root-caused it this morning: the partner's frontends send brand slug 'spinbetterragon' (no hyphen) which matches neither of FT's accepted values ('spinbet-terragon' or the 'vegastars' alias), so 100% of Pusher auth requests 401 -- traffic jumped ~20x on 8 Jul, likely an operator frontend release. Matteo then sent a fully corrected, technically accurate partner message to #ftcrm-mtm at 10:29 CEST explaining the mismatch and asking the partner to confirm the definitive brand slug before FT changes its config.

Open breaches
  • A4: 2nd Fusion API 401 incident in a week (also INC-12617), still not formally escalated as a pattern to the owning team
Proposed / pending
  • Partner asked to check the API exchange and 401 cause on their end - no response received
  • Andrea's direction: investigate new origin/setting mismatch, fix partner comms - just assigned, not yet actioned
Partner view
  • last partner message unanswered
  • will update when more information available - still no follow-up sent to partner
Timeline (8)
  • Morning 15 Jul Joachim re-chases the partner - no reply since
  • 20:22 CEST 15 Jul and prior bot 'mark as resolved?' nudges, unanswered
  • 16 Jul 08:10 CEST Incident Lead reassigned Nazareno -> Joachim (administrative only)
  • 16 Jul 12:48-12:51 CEST Andrea Azzopardi joins the channel directly, tells Joachim this needs more investigation into a possible new origin/setting mismatch, and that partner comms need correcting with the real impact
  • 16 Jul 15:36-15:38 CEST Andrea re-chases again: 'keeping this till tomorrow morning is not ideal'; Joachim admits he hasn't checked yet ('got crazy' in the afternoon); Matteo says he'll look
  • No new activity this cycle (bot nudge only) -- Andrea's 15:36 CEST direction (investigate origin/setting mismatch, fix partner comms) still not actioned as of this check; expected to move this morning
  • 17 Jul 09:34-10:20 CEST Joachim/Matteo root-cause the 401s to a brand-slug mismatch (partner sends 'spinbetterragon', FT accepts 'spinbet-terragon'/'vegastars'); Andrea: 'Nice catch'
  • 17 Jul 10:29 CEST Matteo sends a fully corrected, accurate technical explanation to the partner in #ftcrm-mtm, asking them to confirm the definitive brand slug before any config change -- addresses Andrea's C9/C11 direction from yesterday

→ Once the partner confirms the definitive brand slug, apply the config fix (persist via OneClick EV, not just a manual edit); separately escalate the recurring Fusion API 401 pattern (2nd time in a week) to the owning team, and close INC-12617 as part of the same pattern.

INC-14471 slack intercomP2open 2h· evidence to 17 Jul 08:03 UTC
[P2] Intercom Incident : Rhino Production

Fast SRE pickup and active, ongoing troubleshooting directly with the partner -- underlying bug not yet fixed but no guideline breach given how fresh this is and how engaged the response has been.

Rhino partner (Denis) reported via Intercom on 16 Jul that email previews show header+content instead of just subject/preview, and separately that the FT preview UI flickers. Support (Tara) gave a partial fix the next morning (remove a style causing the preview issue) but the email-preview/flicker problem persisted for several colleagues; escalated to SRE at 10:00 CEST 17 Jul. Joachim picked it up immediately and has been actively troubleshooting with the partner (browser/session checks) since.

Proposed / pending
  • Partial fix (remove flagged style) - applied, resolved one symptom but not the core preview/flicker issue
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-16T13:39
Timeline (4)
  • 16 Jul 13:39-14:29 CEST partner (Denis) reports the issue to support via Intercom chat; Tara investigates internally, says a fix may come the next morning
  • 17 Jul 07:35 CEST Tara gives partner a partial fix (remove a style); partner confirms this addresses one symptom but the preview/flicker issue for other colleagues remains
  • 17 Jul 10:00 CEST escalated to SRE as INC-14471; Joachim joins immediately, tests but doesn't reproduce the flicker from his side
  • 17 Jul 08:08-08:32 UTC (10:08-10:32 CEST) Joachim actively troubleshoots with the partner via Intercom (different browsers, fresh sessions) -- issue still not resolved as of this check
INC-14474 slackP2open 2h· evidence to 17 Jul 09:24 UTC
OneCasino: no consumers on queue onecasino.activitymanager.lf-timeinstance (messages: 45, in: 0/s)

Fast partner comms and quick clearance, but the queue draining doesn't yet explain the underlying 'invalid connection' segment-evaluation error Giancarlo found afterward — worth a closing update once that's understood, otherwise it's simply progressing well.

OneCasino no-consumers alert on queue onecasion.activitymanager.lf-timeinstance. Joachim informed the partner promptly, the underlying alert cleared at 11:09 CEST (queue drained), and Giancarlo has since found an application error (AMQP connection closed while evaluating segment 848 for activity 1389 — 'invalid connection') that may point to the deeper root cause, still being chased even though the immediate backlog is gone.

Open breaches
  • D15/C10: alert cleared but incident still open — no closing update yet confirming root cause or that recurrence risk is addressed
Timeline (4)
  • 17 Jul 08:14 UTC alert fires, Giancarlo accepts, Joachim takes lead
  • 10:53 CEST Joachim informs the partner channel
  • 11:09 CEST attached alert auto-resolves (queue drained)
  • 11:24 CEST Giancarlo posts an app error log ('invalid connection' during segment evaluation) — investigating the underlying cause even though volume cleared

→ Have Giancarlo confirm whether the segment-evaluation error is a one-off or recurring before closing.

INC-14486 slackP2open 10m· evidence to 17 Jul 10:35 UTC
[FIRING:1] AWS RDS CPU Above 95% (for more than 20 minutes) royal-reels aws.rds.cpu-royal-reels Alerting (true p2 aws-rds)

Fast, correct response mechanics (instant ack, lead assigned, Monitoring) — flagged 🟡 only because the handler's own comment surfaces a recurring daily pattern (A4) worth tracking across future runs, not because anything is currently mishandled.

AWS RDS CPU alert (>95% for 20+ min) on royal-reels. Joachim Farrugia acknowledged within seconds, took Incident Lead, and set to Monitoring, noting in-channel that this is a 'daily occurrence around this time'.

Open breaches
  • A4: handler's own note flags this as a recurring daily pattern, not yet escalated for a long-term fix — but only 2 minutes old at check time
Timeline (3)
  • 17 Jul 10:34:12 UTC alert fires, Joachim acknowledges within ~13s
  • 10:35:19 UTC Joachim: 'Daily occurance around this time. Monitoring' (with dashboard screenshot)

→ No action yet — watch across the next few digests for whether this royal-reels RDS CPU spike keeps recurring daily without a scaling/query fix.

INC-5630 slackP3open 78d· evidence to 16 Jul 11:07 UTC
Coinbar SMS Issues with Credit
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: known issue with the partner relating to credit — the partner is aware and we are awaiting their further feedback. Watch-level, not escalate.

Per management note: known partner credit issue, FT awaiting their feedback. Andrea is now actively pushing toward a decision point (disable vs. keep waiting) rather than letting it sit indefinitely.

SMS distributor service has not processed queued messages for all Coinbar brands since before 30 Apr - over 1M messages backlogged. Per management note, this is a known credit-related issue with the partner, who is aware; FT is awaiting their feedback. No new activity this cycle.

Open breaches
  • Awaiting partner feedback (credit issue) — keep a periodic chase cadence so the wait stays deliberate
Timeline (2)
  • 16 Jul 13:04-13:07 CEST Andrea asks Gustav Tjäder for direction on whether to disable the service given it's 'just extra noise'; Gustav has sent the question to the partner and will report back
  • No new activity this cycle -- still awaiting Gustav/partner feedback on disable-vs-wait

→ Await Gustav's partner response; if no credit resolution appears, decide explicitly whether to disable/purge and close.

INC-10810 slackP3open 21d· evidence to 16 Jul 02:02 UTC
12play brands fire late

Substance well in hand — purely a record-hygiene gap, but a real handover risk while someone on a month's leave is listed as owner.

12play/we88 (Momentum) players received rewards 45-50min late due to RDS overload; a real fix landed (4 PRs, 2 DB upgrades). Handover from Daniel (on leave) to Sam Lundborg happened in Slack but incident.io still lists Daniel as Incident Lead and status unchanged at 'Fixing' since 10 Jul. No new activity this cycle.

Open breaches

  • RabbitMQ 4.x cutover coordination now owned by Sam Lundborg

INC-12754 slackP3open 8d· evidence to 15 Jul 13:34 UTC
Campeon Nexto unable to login

Fix is solid and no recurrence reported, but the incident continues to drift without a formal close.

Campeon Nexto users couldn't log in due to a v2/v3 URL-version mismatch. Fix held since 08 Jul; incident.io has sat in 'Fixing' P3 status for 8+ days with no substantive activity this cycle either.

Open breaches
  • D15/C10: incident.io still stuck in 'Fixing' P3 status over a week after the fix was confirmed working, no closing note

  • No new Slack activity this cycle

→ Have Giancarlo Laferla post a short confirmation update and move status to Fixed/Closed.

INC-14284 slackP3open 29h· evidence to 16 Jul 18:15 UTC
Daily Data Science Message Spikes

Well-diagnosed and confirmed no partner impact, which resolves the immediate concern — but this is a known recurring daily pattern with no ETA on the actual fix, exactly the kind of thing guideline A4 wants escalated to a committed backlog item rather than re-alerting indefinitely.

Daily 7-8:30AM/19:00-20:45 Data Science message spikes across multiple brands (SlotV, Crocobet, Fairspin, Corestar, Roobet — 50 alerts in 5 days). Clayton (Data Science) confirmed to Joachim and Andrea (both directly engaged) that this is expected heavy Singularity/Argo batch computation with no partner-facing impact, and that adding pods won't help since the jobs themselves are heavy. No ETA exists for lightening the queries; it's in the backlog.

Open breaches
  • A4: confirmed daily recurring pattern (this is at least the 2nd digest cycle it's persisted across) with no committed fix date, just 'in the backlog'
Proposed / pending
  • Lighten Data Science queries - identified as the real fix, no ETA, in backlog
Timeline (5)
  • 16 Jul 05:23 UTC alert fires (recurring pattern, auto-merged from 2 related incidents)
  • 09:38-09:44 CEST Joachim shares 5-day trend data with Clayton/Jeremy, asks about partner impact and mitigation options
  • 11:09-11:10 CEST Clayton: confirmed no partner-facing impact, heavy expected computation, pod scaling won't help; cc'd Andrea
  • 11:10 CEST Joachim: 'no ETA on this and is in the backlog' for lightening the queries
  • No new activity this cycle (bot nudge only) -- still no ETA/backlog priority for the query-lightening fix

→ Get a backlog priority/ETA from Data Science for the query-lightening work, or formally accept the daily alert noise and tune the alert thresholds instead.

INC-14331 slackP3open 23h· evidence to 17 Jul 10:33 UTC
Leonbets Activity Manager Groups Alerts Spamming

Real progress -- PR is proven effective over 12h and moving to review -- but merge is now explicitly pushed to 'next week', extending the window where the fix could revert on a deploy.

Leonbets activity-manager and activity-manager-lifecycles consumer groups were spamming slow-processing alerts (SOC noise), traced by Matteo to a shared Aurora writer being saturated by full-brand config reloads (leonbets has 598k actions, ~7.9M rows read per pod per reload). Matteo deployed a temporary kubectl-level fix (scoped action loading + longer reload interval), cutting RDS CPU from 94-99% to 40-50% and lag from ~94k/77k to <2k on each group within ~90 minutes of investigation starting. Set to Monitoring at 12:16:59 CEST. As of 17 Jul morning, Matteo confirms PR #490 is performing well (RDS CPU healthy, lag under 5-6k peak over the past 12h) and will move it out of draft for review; Malcolm (given the logic overhaul) is asked to review next week.

Open breaches
  • A2: durable fix (PR #490 merge + EV persistence) still not merged -- review now scheduled for 'next week', so today's kubectl-level changes remain exposed to reverting on the next OneClick deploy until then
Proposed / pending
  • Scoped action loading (PR #490) - applied via kubectl today, durable merge+EV persistence still pending
  • Lifecycles reload interval 120s→300s - applied, durable EV persistence still pending
Timeline (8)
  • 16 Jul 10:47 UTC alert fires (pair of alerts, 2 related incidents auto-merged)
  • 12:47-13:20 CEST Matteo root-causes: DB saturation from unscoped config reload, not a Kafka/consumer issue; two prior scale-up attempts (partitions, pod count) made it worse
  • 13:20-14:09 CEST Matteo drafts and deploys PR #490 (scoped action loading) as a kubectl-level change, plus bumped lifecycles reload interval to 300s
  • 14:16:59 CEST incident updated with full root-cause writeup and measured impact; status → Monitoring
  • Explicit note in the update: today's kubectl-level changes revert on the next OneClick deploy unless PR #490 is merged and the env vars persisted
  • No new human activity this cycle (bot nudge only) -- PR #490 merge / EV persistence still not confirmed done
  • 17 Jul 10:03-10:04 CEST Matteo: PR #490 performing well over past 12h (no alerts, lag <5-6k), moving out of draft; will ask Malcolm to review 'next week' given it's a logic overhaul
  • 17 Jul 12:33 CEST: Matteo downgrades severity P2→P3 as the situation stays stable; status remains Monitoring

→ Ask Malcolm to prioritize reviewing PR #490 sooner than 'next week' given the revert-on-deploy risk; merge and persist the two EV settings as soon as approved.

INC-14423 slackP3open 11h· evidence to 17 Jul 06:33 UTC
Container using an image vulnerable to CVE-2021-45046

Andrea personally closed the ownership gap this morning by assigning Stephen -- ⚠ was unowned workload, now assigned; the CVE fix and incident.io summary are still pending Stephen's follow-through.

Wiz security scan flagged a critical Log4Shell (CVE-2021-45046) finding on the kafka-ui container image (staging cluster, sortenabet namespace) -- runtime-validated, CISA KEV-listed, 100% EPSS. Gerard investigated, found contradictory exposure signals (ASG shows no internet exposure, but some instances open to 0.0.0.0/0), and per the runbook's own guidance ('a false escalation costs minutes, a missed P1 costs far more') escalated to security on-call. Paging misfired (Andrea's escalation expired, Giancarlo paged but silent) but Patrik acknowledged and joined directly, reviewed it personally, then handed the lead back to Gerard and downgraded P1->P3 with 'this can wait until tomorrow.'

Open breaches
  • B6: remediation ownership now assigned (Stephen Riolo, per Andrea) but the log4j-core upgrade itself and a formal summary are still not posted
Proposed / pending
  • Upgrade log4j-core to >=2.12.3 in the kafka-ui image -- identified, not yet actioned, deferred to tomorrow per Patrik
Timeline (6)
  • 16 Jul 23:30 UTC Wiz finding surfaces as an incident; Gerard investigates exposure signals (contradictory: ASG-level no exposure vs some instances open to 0.0.0.0/0)
  • 17 Jul 01:49 CEST Gerard raises P2->P1 and escalates per runbook guidance; Andrea's page expired, Giancarlo paged but didn't ack, Patrik acknowledges and joins
  • 17 Jul 02:20-02:21 CEST Patrik takes the lead briefly, downgrades P1->P3, hands lead back to Gerard: 'this can wait until tomorrow'
  • Open item from the Wiz report: the affected kafka-ui workload (docker.io/provectuslabs/kafka-ui:0.0.9, EOL, no security context) has no assigned owner
  • 17 Jul 07:10 CEST Incident Lead handed over Gérard -> Giancarlo Laferla, picking up the deferred remediation as planned
  • 17 Jul 08:32-08:33 CEST Andrea directly asks Stephen Riolo ('the main person in Security not on vacation') to handle it; Stephen joins the channel, bot prompts for a summary

→ Stephen to post an incident.io summary and schedule the log4j-core upgrade (PR pending) now that he owns this.

INC-14440 slackP3open 8h· evidence to 17 Jul 07:34 UTC
Shuffle Performance Dashboards Issue

The promised office-hours follow-up still hasn't produced an owner -- it's now being discussed between SRE and Cloud without resolution, ~7h after the commitment to the partner.

Shuffle partner (Dylan) reported performance dashboards spinning endlessly when an activity is live / just after it ends. The partner himself flagged it as low urgency ('not a P1 or even p2, but I want it looked into'). Gérard agreed and said it would be investigated during office hours. Four more engineers (James, Joachim, Matteo, Nazareno) joined the channel in a bulk handover a minute later, but no investigation has visibly started since.

Open breaches
  • B5/D12: now ~7h after the office-hours commitment (04:23 CEST) with ownership still being bounced between SRE and Cloud rather than someone picking it up; suggestion to have the partner re-raise via Intercom risks looking like a bounce-back to the partner who already reported it
Partner view
  • last partner message unanswered
  • investigate during office hours - agreed 04:23 CEST, not yet started
Timeline (5)
  • 17 Jul 02:21 CEST incident opened, Gérard as reporter+lead
  • 17 Jul 04:22-04:23 CEST Gérard relays the partner's (Dylan/Shuffle) report and agrees it's low urgency, to be looked into 'during office hours'
  • 17 Jul 04:24 CEST James Hodgson, Joachim Farrugia, Matteo Rapisarda, Nazareno Scibilia all join the channel (bulk handover)
  • No substantive activity since (bot 'add a summary?' nudge only at 04:29 CEST) -- now well into office hours (07:37 CEST) with no visible investigation yet
  • 17 Jul 09:06-09:34 CEST Joachim asks Cloud (Gustav/Soner) whether this is SRE's or another team's to fix; Soner asks who should look into it since it's not a P1 and suggests the partner re-raise via Intercom; Joachim: 'if you think that's the best route forward sure' -- no owner confirmed, no fix started

→ Get a firm decision on whether SRE or Cloud owns this investigation today, rather than suggesting the partner re-file via Intercom; reply to Dylan with an ETA either way.

Security incident: Malicious dashboard creation attempt

private — review directly

INC-13889 slack intercomP4open 3d· evidence to 16 Jul 18:03 UTC

Concrete progress and a clear partner-facing deploy date now exist, but the incident.io record still has no formally assigned Incident Lead — the A1/D15 ownership gap remains even though the technical work is on track.

12Play's Chinese comms profile appeared missing from the backoffice — a display bug only, no player-facing impact. Rewards team has now tested the fix and told the partner it will deploy first thing Monday morning. No Incident Lead role has ever been formally assigned in incident.io.

🟢 Under control 5

5x well-handled: INC-13916 (BrazilBet, Andrea+Patrik made an explicit informed close decision after no partner response), INC-14346 (CF Threat Watch, correct security escalation, false positive), INC-14351 (Stanleybet Romania, textbook RCA), INC-14475 (failed-actions-api, brand new, clean mechanics), INC-14476 (Mystake, fast rigorous root-cause + clear partner comms, P1 in ~4h)

INC-13916 slack intercomP1open 2d· evidence to 17 Jul 07:17 UTC
[P1] Intercom Incident : BrazilBet Production

Well-handled: Head of SRE and CTO made an explicit, informed close-now/reopen-if-needed decision (D12) after confirming no breach on our side and no partner response — textbook handling, not a breach. Still shows active in incident.io pending the close action being executed.

private — review directly

Timeline (1)
  • 17 Jul 07:15-07:17 UTC: Patrik asks Andrea directly whether there's been any partner update; Andrea confirms no concrete update despite asking again, explains no breach on our side already shared with partner, and both agree to close now and reopen if the partner comes back
INC-14476 slack intercomP1open 2h· evidence to 17 Jul 10:31 UTC
[P1] Intercom Incident : Mystake Production

Rigorous, fast root-cause work (P1, ~4h from open to a precise partner-side diagnosis) with clear, specific comms to the partner — no breaches, well handled.

Cocobet (Mystake) blocked users appearing to 'unblock' themselves. Joachim ran a thorough data trace (event tables vs canonical snapshots) and confirmed the true root cause: Cocobet's daily reconciliation feed doesn't include users who should remain blocked, so FT's ~04:20 CEST recon job flips them back to unblocked. Sent the partner a clear, specific technical explanation and ask (check their recon feed setup); incident.io moved Investigating → Waiting on Partner.

Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-17T08:42
  • root-cause explanation and recon-feed check request — delivered 12:18-12:19 CEST
Timeline (4)
  • 17 Jul 08:42 UTC incident opened (Intercom ticket), Joachim takes lead instantly
  • 10:54-11:51 CEST Joachim traces block/unblock event history at the data level, rules out a CRM-side bug
  • 12:18-12:19 CEST Joachim sends partner a precise technical writeup identifying the daily recon job (cron 04:20 CEST) as the mechanism and asks them to check their recon feed setup
  • 12:31 CEST incident update posted, status → Waiting on Partner
INC-14346 slackP2open 22h· evidence to 16 Jul 21:42 UTC
CF Threat Watch: brute-force-suspect,login-success-after-fails on ft-crm.com (41.76.35.208)

Fast ack and correct B6 security escalation flow (SOC→SRE→security team), even though the paging itself misfired (Max unavailable, Agustín's escalation expired) — both engaged within ~4 minutes regardless. Too fresh and too well-handled to flag; current evidence points to a false positive.

CF Threat Watch flagged a brute-force-suspect pattern (401×37 then 40×2xx SUCCESS logins) from a single IP on ft-crm.com. Joachim acknowledged instantly; escalation to the security on-call (Max, Agustín) had paging misses (Max unavailable, Agustín's escalation expired) but both joined the channel manually within ~4 minutes anyway via Stephen Riolo, who took over as lead. Team is jointly investigating via Cloudflare security analytics; current read is a shared-network false positive, not an attack — CF itself classifies it as 'not an exploit'.

Proposed / pending
  • Verify via Cloudflare security analytics + bot session double-check - in progress
Timeline (5)
  • 16 Jul 12:10:44 UTC alert fires, Joachim acknowledges within seconds
  • 12:11:36-12:14:59 UTC escalation to security on-call: Max paged but unavailable, Agustín's escalation expired, Stephen Riolo acknowledges and becomes lead; Max and Agustín both join the channel regardless
  • 12:21-12:39 UTC joint investigation via Cloudflare dashboard; Agustín: 'doesn't look like an attack and CF is classifying it as not an exploit'; Stephen: 'don't think this is an attack... more some shared network'
  • Still actively investigating at check time (~29min old)
  • 16 Jul 23:42 CEST bot nudge asking to mark resolved -- no human response yet (overnight, expected)
INC-14351 slack intercomP2open 22h· evidence to 17 Jul 06:37 UTC
[P2] Intercom Incident : Stanleybet Romania Production

Textbook handling -- root cause traced precisely end-to-end with quantified impact, a fix already in review, real consent confirmed untouched, and the partner independently agrees there's no real issue. Upgraded 🟡→🟢.

Stanleybet Romania (partner) reported users showing as 'unsubscribed' in activity reporting despite being subscribed. Giancarlo confirmed within the hour it's a false alarm: automated email-security scanners/link-prefetchers auto-POST the RFC 8058 one-click-unsubscribe link while scanning inbound mail (no human click), which Stanleybet's self-hosted unsubscribe page treats as a real opt-out and forwards to prisma-api, which in turn feeds the reporting pipeline (falsely flagging 74,234 brand-206 users) while leaving real consent untouched (confirmed against the operator, Comtrade, which still shows opted-in). Fix PR #106 (gate the legacy handler so the enriched/authoritative consent path wins) is in review. Partner has independently confirmed on their side that they see no real unsubscribe problem, only the reporting glitch.

Proposed / pending
  • Fix the consent-event opt-in/opt-out check so it stops miscounting as unsubscribe — root cause identified, fix not yet applied
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-15T13:07
  • Root cause (false-positive reporting bug) explained internally 16 Jul; partner has now separately confirmed they see no real issue on their end, only the reporting glitch
Timeline (9)
  • 15 Jul 13:07 UTC partner (Marius) first reports the mismatch via Intercom support
  • 16 Jul 12:41 UTC Tara (support) escalates to SRE after internal checks
  • 16 Jul 12:51-13:47 CEST Giancarlo investigates, finds it's a reporting false-positive (consent-direction check bug), still pending final partner confirmation
  • 16 Jul 14:47 CEST Andrea flags a possible pattern link to INC-13738 (Betiro no-unsubs) and a separate informal Slack discussion
  • 16 Jul 16:33 CEST Incident Lead handed over Joachim → James Hodgson
  • 16 Jul 15:02 CEST partner (Marius) confirms: 'seeing how we are managing the unsubscribe function ourselves, I don't think it's an issue. The only issue we're seeing is the faulty reporting' — partner side fully satisfied
  • 16 Jul 23:25 CEST Incident Lead handed over James Hodgson -> Gerard E. Pelayo (no substantive activity since)
  • 17 Jul 08:12-08:16 CEST Giancarlo confirms false alarm; posts a full end-to-end technical write-up (scanner/prefetch auto-POST -> prisma-api hardcodes opt-out -> conversion-service misreads it as email_unsubscribed) with PR #106 already in review, plus data-backfill and upstream-hardening follow-ups noted
  • 17 Jul 08:15 CEST Incident Lead handed to Joachim; 08:37 CEST formal incident.io update posted with the full root-cause summary
INC-14475 slackP4open 2h· evidence to 17 Jul 08:40 UTC
failed-actions-api crashing

1 minute old at check time -- lead already assigned, too fresh to judge on anything else.

failed-actions-api crashing across multiple brands (betpipo, padisahbet, play, mb8, h2bet, betfast, betoffice, luckyriches, galabet). Reported by Giancarlo, lead assigned to Joachim immediately.

Timeline (2)
  • 17 Jul 08:39:55 UTC incident opened, Incident Lead assigned (Joachim Farrugia) immediately
  • 17 Jul 10:40 CEST: Incident Lead reassigned from Giancarlo (reporter) to Joachim Farrugia
